text: Either party may terminate the agreement.
The supplier shall deliver the goods in thirty days.
Each party must respect every clause.
The parties must not share confidential data.
Rafael, before we sign, can you explain the main obligations of this contract?
Of course. The supplier shall deliver the goods within thirty days, and the client must pay each invoice within fifteen days.
Good. And may either party share the information with another company?
No. Clause three says the parties must not share confidential information with a third company.
And what happens if one party breaks a clause?
That is a breach of contract. In that case, the other party may terminate the agreement with written notice.

Good afternoon. Before you sign, let me explain the main terms of this contract. The agreement is valid for two years from the signing date. Both parties must respect every clause. The supplier shall deliver the goods within thirty days of each order. The client must pay each invoice within fifteen days. Neither party may share confidential information with a third company. If one party breaks a clause, that is a breach of contract, and the agreement allows the other party to terminate it with a written notice. Please read every term carefully. When you are ready, we can sign. Do you have any questions about the obligations?

In my years as a lawyer, I have read thousands of contracts, and I always tell my clients the same thing. A contract is a promise in writing. Every clause is there for a reason. The word shall means a strong duty: the company shall pay, the supplier shall deliver. The word may means permission, not duty: the client may cancel, but does not have to. And must not means it is forbidden: you must not share the data. When both parties understand their obligations, the agreement protects everyone. A good contract is not about distrust. It is about being clear. So read every term, ask questions, and never sign a clause you do not understand.

First, both parties read every clause of the contract carefully. Then, the lawyer explains the main obligations and terms. Next, each party checks that the agreement is valid and fair. After that, both parties sign the contract on the signing date. Finally, if one party breaks a clause, the other party may terminate the agreement.
The supplier shall deliver the goods within thirty days.
The client must pay each invoice within fifteen days.
Either party may terminate the agreement.
The parties must not share confidential information.
A late payment is a breach of contract.
The client must pay each invoice.
